sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, I can see the black shape has moved right and now overlaps more with the yellow shape. The yellow shape's horizontal part is still visible on the right side. Let me check the segmentation to see the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5 and n['color'] not in ('b', 'S'):
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W':
        print(f"  WHITE: id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'Y' and n['pixels'] < 40:
        print(f"  YELLOW: id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

print(f"\nTimer yellow: {seg['nodes'][1]['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

To make the black shape overlap with the remaining yellow horizontal part, I need to move the black shape right by 1 column. But pressing RIGHT moves the black shape right by 3 columns, which would put it at cols 51-59, overlapping with the yellow at cols 57-59.
